title: Migration / Irregular Immigration Flow Panel
summary: Tracks migration, irregular or unauthorized border movement, and crime-data source splits without pretending every dataset measures the same thing.
evidenceRule: A border encounter, asylum application, removal, return, visa-overstay estimate, unauthorized-population estimate, and illegal-entry estimate are different categories. Encounters are not unique people. Nationality is not immigration status. Police suspects are not convictions. Total sexual-offence statistics exist in official national datasets, but the immigration-status split is usually separate, partial, or unavailable.

flowPanels.publicFigure: FY2025 Southwest Border marker: about 237.5K apprehensions / encounters; 2023 unauthorized-population estimates range around 13.7M to 14.0M depending method.
flowPanels.categoryWarning: Encounters are not people. Apprehensions, inadmissibles, removals, returns, asylum claims, visa overstays, and unauthorized-population estimates must be kept separate.
flowPanels.confidence: Medium: current public-record lane, categories not directly comparable

flowPanels.publicFigure: EU external irregular-border detections: about 178K in 2025; UK small-boat arrivals: 41,472 in 2025; EU/UK asylum applications: about 770K in 2025 in public reporting.
flowPanels.categoryWarning: Frontex detections are not unique people. Asylum applications, returns, removals, Channel crossings, overstays, and country-level flows are separate categories.
flowPanels.sourceLabel: Frontex / Eurostat / Home Office / Reuters / Guardian
flowPanels.confidence: Medium: official source lanes identified, latest figures need weekly refresh

flowPanels.publicFigure: Central Mediterranean dead/missing marker: 800+ reported dead or missing from January to mid-May 2026; more than 1,300 reported in 2025.
flowPanels.categoryWarning: Deaths and missing persons are route-specific and undercounted. Desert routes and invisible shipwrecks are harder to verify.
flowPanels.sourceLabel: IOM Missing Migrants / AP / humanitarian monitors
flowPanels.confidence: Medium-low: official minimum counts; true toll likely higher

flowPanels.publicFigure: Global displacement marker: roughly 117M to 123.2M depending date and category; latest public reporting cites 117M displaced by war/persecution at mid-2025 and 123.2M forcibly displaced at end-2024.
flowPanels.categoryWarning: Refugees, asylum seekers, internally displaced people, stateless people, returnees, irregular migrants, and unauthorized residents are different categories.
flowPanels.sourceLabel: UNHCR / IOM / Reuters / official humanitarian datasets
flowPanels.confidence: Medium: headline total changes by definition and date

sexualAssaultCountryLane.title: Sexual Offence Statistics / Migrant-Status Split By Country
sexualAssaultCountryLane.summary: Every listed country has official sexual-offence or sexual-violence statistics. The missing piece is not the existence of sexual-assault figures; it is the clean split by immigration status, asylum status, irregular status, suspect stage, charge stage, and conviction stage.
sexualAssaultCountryLane.euHeadline: Eurostat 2024 EU marker: 256,302 police-recorded sexual violence offences, including 98,190 rape offences. These are police-recorded offences, not immigration-status splits.
sexualAssaultCountryLane.evidenceRule: Official sexual-offence totals exist. Do not infer immigration status from nationality. Do not treat police suspects as convictions. Do not turn individual cases into group guilt. Unknown-status cases remain unknown. Police-recorded offences, suspects, charges, prosecutions, convictions, prisoners, nationality, citizenship, foreign-born status, asylum status, and irregular status are different datasets.
statusLegend: NATIONALITY / CITIZENSHIP SPLIT AVAILABLE OR PARTIAL
CONVICTION SPLIT NEEDS SEPARATE COURT SOURCE

countries.officialStatsLane: ONS / Home Office police recorded crime / MoJ courts
countries.latestSourceStatus: Official total sexual-offence and rape data available through national crime releases; migration-status split is not a single comparable official table.
countries.sourceLabel: ONS / Home Office / MoJ / police force data / courts
countries.evidenceWarning: Foreign national, foreign-born, suspect, charged, convicted, asylum seeker, and irregular migrant are separate categories.

countries.latestSourceStatus: Official crime and justice lanes exist; migrant-status split needs exact source extraction and category labels.
